name: SSO Authentication
description:
This plugin allows users to sign in through an SSO provider (such as Google, Facebook, or your own provider). This enables one-click signin.
Review documentation at https://github.com/Derksniff/jellyfin-plugin-sso
overview: Authenticate users against an SSO provider.

versions.changelog: First official release of the fork. SECURITY: logins are keyed on the immutable subject (OIDC sub / SAML NameID) and no longer auto-claim an existing account by username - set enableUnverifiedLinking to restore the old behavior (existing links migrate automatically on next login). SAML hardened (single-use assertions, strict validity windows, optional audience check, role enforcement on the auth endpoint). Avatar fetches are SSRF-guarded. Logging, concurrency, and robustness fixes; added tests and CI.

The loading page now renders before the OpenID token exchange, so users see "Connecting to your account..." immediately instead of a blank tab while the provider is contacted. Login logo replaced with a padlock icon.
